description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a robust, dark metallic handle and two large, symmetrical blades. The blades are forged from a silvery metal with a rugged, hammered texture and feature intricate copper-colored inlays forming geometric patterns. The axe head is topped with a pointed spike, adding to its menacing appearance. The handle is cylindrical, reinforced with metal bands, and ends in a heavy pommel with a ring for attachment or hanging. The overall design is ornate and suggests a ceremonial or fantasy-inspired weapon, rather than a utilitarian tool. The materials and craftsmanship give it a heavy, durable, and intimidating presence.
description: A double-headed, ornate battle axe with hammered silver blades, copper inlays, and a reinforced dark metal handle ending in a ringed pommel.
description_view_0: Front view showing both symmetrical blades with copper inlays and the full handle with its decorative pommel.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the pointed spike at the top, with minimal blade width visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the back of the blades and handle, with similar decorative details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin edge of the blades and the pointed spike, similar to view 1.
attribute_handle_type: Cylindrical, straight, dark metallic handle with reinforced bands and a ringed pommel; likely metal-wrapped wood for grip.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, symmetrical, wide crescent blades with hammered texture, copper inlays, and a central pointed spike; no beard or poll.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and banded mounting with visible reinforcement at the head-handle junction.
